The health benefits of this plant include its potential to treat various inflammatory conditions, improve respiratory function, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. taxonomy: Dryopteris odontoloma is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is a type of shield fern that is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the world. The classification of Dryopteris odontoloma is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Division: Pteridophyta, Class: Polypodiopsida, Order: Polypodiales, Family: Dryopteridaceae, Genus: Dryopteris, Species: D. odontoloma. This species is part of the larger genus Dryopteris, which includes many other species of shield ferns. The classification of Dryopteris odontoloma is consistent with the APG III system, which is a widely accepted classification system for plants.
